subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Demonstrates strong ability to develop original concepts, supported by clear research and well-defined narrative intent.
refined: Screenwriting: Develops original concepts with confidence, supported by clear research and well-defined narrative intent.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Shows a clear understanding of story fundamentals, including premise, conflict, and character motivation, with effective application.
refined: Screenwriting: Shows a clear grasp of story fundamentals — premise, conflict, and character motivation — and applies them effectively.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Writes with clarity and visual awareness, translating ideas into scenes that are cinematic and engaging.
refined: Screenwriting: Writes with clarity and visual awareness, translating ideas into cinematic, engaging scenes.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Demonstrates strong control over basic story structure, creating coherent narratives using the three-act framework.
refined: Screenwriting: Demonstrates strong control over basic story structure, creating coherent narratives using the three-act framework.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Shows originality and depth in creative writing exercises, with a distinct narrative voice and perspective.
refined: Screenwriting: Brings originality and depth to creative writing exercises, with a distinct narrative voice and perspective.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Effectively develops characters with clear motivations that contribute to meaningful narrative progression.
refined: Screenwriting: Develops characters with clear motivations that drive meaningful narrative progression.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Demonstrates strong observational skills, translating real-life experiences into compelling story ideas.
refined: Screenwriting: Observes keenly, translating real-life experiences into compelling story ideas.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Actively engages with reading and analysis of stories, applying insights to their own writing practice
refined: Screenwriting: Actively reads and analyses stories, and applies those insights to their own writing practice — a habit that will keep deepening their craft as the reading widens.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Shows a good understanding of concept development, though ideas require further refinement and clarity.
refined: Screenwriting: Understands concept development well. Advice to refine each idea to a single clear premise line and pressure-test it for focus — clearer, sharper concepts come through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Demonstrates familiarity with story fundamentals, with scope to further strengthen consistency in handling conflict and character motivation.
refined: Screenwriting: Is familiar with story fundamentals. Advice to anchor every scene in a clear character want and a clear obstacle so conflict and motivation stay consistent — this consistency comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Writes with basic clarity, and can further enhance scenes by developing stronger visual and cinematic detail.
refined: Screenwriting: Writes with clear, readable scenes. Advice to develop stronger cinematic detail by describing what we see and hear in concrete, specific images — visual writing sharpens through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Understands the three-act structure, though narrative progression can be developed further to feel more engaging and dynamic.
refined: Screenwriting: Understands the three-act structure. Advice to strengthen the act turns and raise stakes at each so progression feels dynamic and engaging — this comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Shows effort in creative writing, with scope to further strengthen originality and depth.
refined: Screenwriting: Shows real effort in creative writing. Advice to push past the first idea and ground writing in specific, lived detail — stronger originality and depth come through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Develops characters with some clarity, with scope to build stronger individuality and depth.
refined: Screenwriting: Develops characters with some clarity. Advice to build individuality through specific backstory, contradictions, and a personal want — distinctive, layered characters come through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Engages with observational exercises, though translation into strong story ideas needs development.
refined: Screenwriting: Engages with observational exercises. Advice to turn observations into stories by asking what conflict or change each moment could spark — translating observation into ideas strengthens through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Demonstrates engagement with reading tasks, though application of insights into writing is still developing.
refined: Screenwriting: Engages with reading tasks. Advice to turn each reading into one concrete technique to try in the next draft — applying insights to your own writing strengthens through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Shows developing clarity in concept development, though ideas still need stronger focus and refinement.
refined: Screenwriting: Concept development is becoming clearer. Advice to distil each idea to a single sharp premise line and pressure-test it for focus — clearer concepts come through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Shows partial understanding of story fundamentals, with scope to strengthen clarity in conflict development.
refined: Screenwriting: Has a partial grasp of story fundamentals. Advice to define a clear central conflict — who wants what, against what — and develop it scene by scene, which strengthens through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Writing shows developing visual clarity, with scope to better translate ideas into cinematic scenes.
refined: Screenwriting: Visual clarity is developing. Advice to translate ideas into concrete images we can see and hear on screen, scene by scene — cinematic writing sharpens through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Shows developing understanding of story structure, though narratives may feel uneven or incomplete at times.
refined: Screenwriting: Shows a developing grasp of story structure. Advice to outline each story against the three-act framework before drafting, so set-up, conflict and resolution land clearly — structure becomes second nature with repeated practice.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Creative writing exercises show scope for further development in originality and depth, moving beyond predictable ideas.
refined: Screenwriting: Creative writing exercises are taking shape. Advice to push past the first, predictable idea and ground writing in specific, lived detail — originality and depth come through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Characters show emerging development, though motivations and narrative contribution can be strengthened.
refined: Screenwriting: Characters are starting to take shape. Advice to give each a clear want and a stake in the outcome so their motivations drive the story — well-motivated characters come through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Observational exercises show potential, though translation into usable story material needs further development.
refined: Screenwriting: Observational exercises show potential. Advice to ask what conflict or change each observed moment could spark, turning notes into scenes — translating observation into story material strengthens through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Shows developing engagement with reading and analysis tasks, with scope to further strengthen application in writing.
refined: Screenwriting: Is engaging more with reading and analysis. Advice to convert each reading into one concrete technique to try in the next draft — applying insights to your writing strengthens through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Shows emerging understanding of concept development, though ideas require further clarity and completion.
refined: Screenwriting: Concept development is beginning to form. Advice to distil each idea to a single clear premise line and carry it through to a complete piece — clear, finished concepts come through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Shows emerging familiarity with basic story elements such as premise, conflict, and character, with scope for stronger understanding.
refined: Screenwriting: Is becoming familiar with the basics of premise, conflict, and character. Advice to plan each of these clearly before drafting a scene — a firmer grasp of story elements comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Writing shows limited clarity and needs further development to achieve cinematic readability as a screenplay.
refined: Screenwriting: Writing is developing toward screenplay form. Advice to write in concrete present-tense action describing only what we see and hear, in standard format — cinematic readability comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Shows early-stage understanding of basic story structure, with scope for further development.
refined: Screenwriting: Shows an early grasp of basic story structure. Advice to outline each story as set-up, conflict, and resolution before drafting — a sense of structure grows through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Creative writing exercises show scope for greater completion and development of ideas.
refined: Screenwriting: Creative writing exercises are getting under way. Advice to carry each idea through to a full, finished piece and develop it with specific detail — completion and depth come through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Characters show early-stage development and need further clarity in definition and purpose.
refined: Screenwriting: Characters are at an early stage. Advice to define each one's want, role in the story, and a defining trait so their purpose is clear — well-drawn characters come through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Observational and ideation exercises show initial engagement, with scope for further development.
refined: Screenwriting: Is beginning to engage with observation and ideation. Advice to ask what conflict or change each observed moment could spark and develop the strongest into scenes — turning observation into ideas grows through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Engagement with reading and analysis tasks is developing, with scope to strengthen participation and application.
refined: Screenwriting: Engagement with reading and analysis is developing. Advice to take part actively and turn each reading into one technique to try in your own writing — application strengthens through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Writes dialogue that is natural, character-driven, and effectively reveals subtext with minimal reliance on exposition.
refined: Screenwriting: Writes natural, character-driven dialogue that reveals subtext with minimal reliance on exposition.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Demonstrates strong control over scene writing, with clear progression, conflict, and purpose in each scene.
refined: Screenwriting: Demonstrates strong control over scene writing, with clear progression, conflict, and purpose in every scene.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Applies advanced story structures (Hero’s Journey / Story Circle) effectively to build engaging and well-shaped narratives.
refined: Screenwriting: Applies advanced structures such as the Hero's Journey and Story Circle effectively to build engaging, well-shaped narratives.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Shows strong understanding of conflict, creating tension that drives the narrative forward.
refined: Screenwriting: Understands conflict well, creating tension that consistently drives the narrative forward.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Demonstrates distinct character voices, making dialogue identifiable and unique to each character.
refined: Screenwriting: Writes distinct character voices, making dialogue identifiable and unique to each character — a real strength to keep stretching with more varied roles.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Writes with strong awareness of rhythm and pacing, allowing scenes to flow naturally and effectively.
refined: Screenwriting: Writes with strong awareness of rhythm and pacing, letting scenes flow naturally and effectively.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Effectively uses dialogue across different situations, adapting tone and style to suit context and character.
refined: Screenwriting: Uses dialogue effectively across situations, adapting tone and style to suit context and character.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Integrates writing with visual thinking, ensuring scenes are cinematic and performable.
refined: Screenwriting: Integrates writing with visual thinking, ensuring scenes are cinematic and performable.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Writes dialogue that serves the narrative, though it can sometimes become predictable or overly explanatory.
refined: Screenwriting: Writes dialogue that serves the narrative. Advice to let characters speak indirectly and withhold what's obvious so lines feel less predictable and explanatory — natural, layered dialogue comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Demonstrates an understanding of scene construction, though progression and conflict can be further strengthened.
refined: Screenwriting: Understands the basics of scene construction. Advice to give each scene a clear want, an obstacle, and a turn so progression and conflict strengthen — purposeful scenes come through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Applies story structures appropriately, though narratives can be developed further for greater depth and originality.
refined: Screenwriting: Applies story structures appropriately. Advice to push each idea past the obvious turn — develop greater depth and originality by drafting two or three alternative versions of key beats, which sharpens through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Shows awareness of conflict, though it can be developed more consistently across the script.
refined: Screenwriting: Shows awareness of conflict. Advice to ensure every scene carries some friction, internal or external, so tension runs throughout — consistent conflict comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Characters show some distinction, though dialogue voices can be further differentiated.
refined: Screenwriting: Characters show some distinction. Advice to develop stronger individual voices by writing a short solo speech for each character — distinct, identifiable dialogue comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Demonstrates basic control over pacing, though scenes can feel uneven at times.
refined: Screenwriting: Has basic control over pacing. Advice to even out the rhythm by trimming scenes that linger and giving key beats room to breathe — smooth pacing comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Writes dialogue suited to situations, though there is scope for greater tonal variation.
refined: Screenwriting: Writes dialogue that suits the situation. Advice to vary tone with the emotional temperature of each scene and each character's state — tonal range in dialogue comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Shows an understanding of cinematic writing, though execution can be strengthened for greater impact.
refined: Screenwriting: Understands cinematic writing. Advice to strengthen execution by choosing the most telling image for each beat and writing it in concrete, specific terms — impactful visual writing comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Dialogue shows developing depth, though it can sometimes become functional or overly explanatory. Focus on subtext and character-specific speech patterns so conversations feel more natural and emotionally layered.
refined: Screenwriting: Dialogue is gaining depth. Advice to lean on subtext and give each character their own speech pattern so conversations feel natural and emotionally layered — this develops through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Scene writing shows developing consistency, though progression and purpose can be made clearer.
refined: Screenwriting: Scene writing is becoming more consistent. Advice to give each scene a clear purpose and a turn that moves the story on — purposeful progression comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Shows developing understanding of story structures, with scope to strengthen narrative flow.
refined: Screenwriting: Shows a developing grasp of story structures. Advice to outline beats in order and check each leads into the next so the narrative flows — smooth flow comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Conflict shows emerging development, though clarity and consistency can be further strengthened.
refined: Screenwriting: Conflict is beginning to emerge. Advice to define what each character wants and what blocks them, then keep that friction alive in every scene — clear, consistent conflict comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Characters show developing voices, though dialogue can sometimes feel repetitive and needs more distinction.
refined: Screenwriting: Character voices are developing. Advice to give each character a distinct vocabulary, rhythm, and way of avoiding the point — distinct, non-repetitive voices come through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting

original: Screenwriting: Dialogue shows developing alignment with situation and character intent, with scope for greater clarity.
refined: Screenwriting: Dialogue is starting to fit situation and character intent. Advice to clarify what each character wants from a line before writing it — clearer, purposeful dialogue comes through repeated practice and feedback.

refined: Screenwriting: Cinematic quality is emerging. Advice to visualise each scene as concrete action and to write moments actors can play — clearer, more performable scenes come through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Dialogue shows emerging clarity, though it needs further development in purpose and character distinction.
refined: Screenwriting: Dialogue is becoming clearer. Advice to give every line a purpose and each character a distinct way of speaking — purposeful, distinctive dialogue comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Scene writing shows emerging structure, with scope to develop clearer beginnings, progression, and resolution.
refined: Screenwriting: Scene writing is starting to take structure. Advice to give each scene a clear opening, a development through conflict, and a turn or resolution — well-shaped scenes come through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Shows emerging understanding of story structures, with scope to strengthen narrative progression.
refined: Screenwriting: Shows an emerging grasp of story structures. Advice to outline beats in order and check each leads logically into the next — strong narrative progression comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Conflict shows emerging development, though it needs clearer articulation and consistency.
refined: Screenwriting: Conflict is beginning to emerge. Advice to state plainly what each character wants and what blocks them, then keep that tension running through every scene — clear, consistent conflict comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Characters show early-stage development, with scope to build clearer voice and motivation.
refined: Screenwriting: Characters are at an early stage. Advice to give each a clear motivation and a distinct voice through specific word choice and rhythm — voice and motivation strengthen through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Pacing shows emerging control, though writing may feel disjointed at times. Practice restructuring scenes and tightening transitions so the narrative flow feels smoother and more engaging.
refined: Screenwriting: Pacing is starting to come under control. Advice to restructure scenes and tighten transitions so the writing flows rather than feeling disjointed — smooth pacing comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Dialogue shows developing alignment with situation and character, with scope to improve credibility and clarity.
refined: Screenwriting: Dialogue is starting to fit situation and character. Advice to read lines aloud and ask what each character truly wants from them — credible, clear dialogue comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Shows developing engagement with basic screenwriting skills required for the course, with scope for further improvement.
refined: Screenwriting: Is engaging with the basic screenwriting skills. Advice to practise the fundamentals — premise, conflict, scene structure, and visual writing — a little each week, as these build steadily through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Demonstrates strong command over narrative theory, effectively applying story vs discourse, character arcs, and archetypes. Structural frameworks are used with clarity and precision, while dialogue remains economical and character-specific, revealing subtext naturally.emains economical and character-specific, revealing subtext without overstatement.
refined: Screenwriting: Commands narrative theory well, applying story vs discourse, character arcs, and archetypes with clarity and precision, while keeping dialogue economical and character-specific so subtext emerges naturally.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Shows clear control over structure using frameworks such as the Fichtean Curve and Seven-Point Structure. Scenes are visually driven, supported by strong pre-visualisation and purposeful dialogue.
refined: Screenwriting: Controls structure clearly through frameworks such as the Fichtean Curve and Seven-Point Structure, with visually driven scenes, strong pre-visualisation, and purposeful dialogue.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Integrates theme and premise seamlessly into scene construction, with each beat contributing to both narrative and character progression. Subtext is consistently present, with underlying conflict shaping interactions.
refined: Screenwriting: Integrates theme and premise seamlessly into scene construction, with every beat advancing both story and character, and subtext consistently shaping interactions.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Demonstrates advanced understanding of genre conventions, using them with originality and confidence. Dialogue, action, and visual elements work together to create layered storytelling.
refined: Screenwriting: Uses genre conventions with originality and confidence, weaving dialogue, action, and visual elements together into layered storytelling.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Balances character-driven and plot-driven storytelling effectively, with well-defined arcs and purposeful escalation. Dialogue uses rhythm, pauses, and interruption to enhance emotional beats.
refined: Screenwriting: Balances character- and plot-driven storytelling with well-defined arcs and purposeful escalation, and uses rhythm, pauses, and interruption in dialogue to enhance emotional beats.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Applies research and development rigorously, resulting in well-realised settings and coherent worlds. Visual writing is precise, with clear staging and cinematic clarity.
refined: Screenwriting: Researches and develops rigorously, producing well-realised settings and coherent worlds, with precise visual writing and clear, cinematic staging.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Scene construction is strong, with clear conflict, progression, and emotional layering. Subtext emerges effectively through behaviour and interaction.
refined: Screenwriting: Constructs strong scenes with clear conflict, progression, and emotional layering, letting subtext emerge effectively through behaviour and interaction.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Overall work reflects high-level integration of narrative theory, structure, genre, and writing craft. Dialogue, subtext, and visual storytelling are consistently controlled and purposeful.
refined: Screenwriting: Overall work integrates narrative theory, structure, genre, and craft at a high level, with consistently controlled, purposeful dialogue, subtext, and visual storytelling.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Shows good understanding of narrative principles and character arcs, though application of story vs discourse can be developed more consistently. Dialogue is functional, with scope to reduce reliance on direct exposition.
refined: Screenwriting: Understands narrative principles and character arcs well. Advice to apply story vs discourse more consistently by varying how events are revealed, and to cut direct exposition in favour of implication — this develops through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Demonstrates familiarity with structural frameworks, though progression can be refined further for greater precision. Visual writing supports the narrative and can be developed with more cinematic specificity.
refined: Screenwriting: Is familiar with structural frameworks. Advice to outline beats precisely before drafting for cleaner progression, and to sharpen visual writing with specific, concrete images of what we see and hear — both develop through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Overall work is competent and structured, with clear scope for deeper integration between narrative theory, structure, and expressive writing techniques.
refined: Screenwriting: Overall work is competent and well-structured. Advice to integrate theory, structure, and expressive technique by letting structural choices serve theme and character in each draft — this integration deepens through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Theme and premise are present, with scope to strengthen their integration into scenes and character decisions. Subtext appears in moments and can be sustained more consistently.
refined: Screenwriting: Theme and premise are present. Advice to let them shape concrete character decisions in each scene and to sustain subtext throughout rather than in moments — this integration comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Shows awareness of genre conventions, though greater experimentation can help strengthen originality. Some scenes can rely more confidently on visual storytelling.
refined: Screenwriting: Shows awareness of genre conventions. Advice to play with and subvert those conventions for originality, and to let more scenes carry meaning through image rather than dialogue — both develop through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Character arcs are identifiable, with scope to build greater depth and consistency. Dialogue reflects intent and can further differentiate character voices.
refined: Screenwriting: Character arcs are identifiable. Advice to map each character's want and change across the script for greater depth and consistency, and to give each a distinct vocabulary and rhythm so their voices differentiate clearly.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Scene construction supports narrative progression, though pacing and escalation can be refined further. Emotional beats can be conveyed more through implication and behaviour.
refined: Screenwriting: Scene construction supports narrative progression. Advice to sharpen pacing and escalation by raising stakes each scene, and to convey emotion through behaviour and implication rather than statement — this comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Research and development are evident, with scope to further strengthen world-building and pre-visualisation. Visual elements are present and can be utilised more effectively.
refined: Screenwriting: Research and development are evident. Advice to deepen world-building with specific sensory detail and to pre-visualise scenes shot by shot so visual elements work harder — both strengthen through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Demonstrates basic understanding of narrative concepts, though distinctions between story and discourse are still developing in execution. Dialogue remains largely functional and can be further strengthened with clearer character specificity.
refined: Screenwriting: Has a basic grasp of narrative concepts. Advice to practise varying how events are revealed (discourse) versus what happens (story), and to make dialogue character-specific — both develop through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Attempts to apply structural frameworks, though narrative progression can feel uneven or incomplete in parts. Scenes can rely more on visual action alongside dialogue.
refined: Screenwriting: Is beginning to apply structural frameworks. Advice to outline beats against the framework before drafting for even progression, and to add visual action alongside dialogue — both strengthen through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Theme and premise show emerging development, with scope to influence narrative direction more clearly. Subtext is present in parts and can be developed more consistently beyond literal interaction.
refined: Screenwriting: Theme and premise are starting to emerge. Advice to let them steer concrete story choices and to sustain subtext beyond literal exchanges — clearer thematic direction comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Needs to further expand understanding of genre conventions to achieve stronger tonal consistency and clearer genre identity.
refined: Screenwriting: Is building an understanding of genre. Advice to study the tonal and structural conventions of the target genre and apply them consistently for a clear genre identity — this comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Character arcs show developing progression, though transformation and consistency can be strengthened further. Dialogue can express emotions more through interaction and behaviour.
refined: Screenwriting: Character arcs are developing. Advice to define each character's start and end point so transformation reads clearly and consistently, and to show emotion through behaviour rather than statement — this comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Scenes are functional, with scope to strengthen conflict, progression, and dramatic purpose. Emotional beats can be constructed more effectively.
refined: Screenwriting: Scenes are functional. Advice to build each around a want, an obstacle, and a turn, and to stage emotional beats through behaviour — stronger dramatic purpose comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Research and development show early-stage engagement, with scope to strengthen settings, visualisation, and cinematic expression beyond literary description.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Overall work meets basic requirements, with scope to further strengthen clarity, structure, and consistency across narrative, genre, and writing craft elements.
refined: Screenwriting: Overall work meets the basic requirements. Advice to outline clearly before drafting and revise for consistency across narrative, genre, and craft — stronger structure and clarity come through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting:  Shows emerging understanding of narrative structure, with scope to strengthen distinctions between story progression and narrative presentation. Dialogue can become less explanatory and more dramatically motivated with further practice.
refined: Screenwriting: Is beginning to understand narrative structure. Advice to separate what happens (story) from how it is revealed (presentation), and to let dialogue come from character need rather than explanation — both grow steadily through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Overall work would benefit from deeper engagement with Semester III concepts, particularly in narrative construction, dialogue writing, and visual storytelling.
refined: Screenwriting: Is building the Semester III skills. Advice to focus on constructing scenes with clear conflict, writing character-driven dialogue, and showing story through image — these strengthen together through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Needs further practice applying basic structural frameworks to create smoother narrative progression and more coherent scene construction.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Would benefit from clearer articulation and integration of theme and premise, with scope to strengthen the use of subtext within scenes and character interactions.
refined: Screenwriting: Is beginning to work with theme and premise. Advice to state both clearly and let them shape concrete scene choices, and to build subtext into interactions — this integration comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Needs stronger understanding and application of genre conventions to achieve greater tonal consistency and storytelling impact.
refined: Screenwriting: Is building an understanding of genre. Advice to study the tonal and structural conventions of the chosen genre and apply them consistently — tonal control and impact come through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Character development would benefit from clearer arcs and motivations, while dialogue can further differentiate character intent and personality.
refined: Screenwriting: Character development is beginning to form. Advice to give each character a clear want and a change to undergo, and a distinct way of speaking — well-defined, individual characters come through repeated practice and feedback.

original: Screenwriting: Needs further practice in structuring scenes, conflict, and dramatic purpose to improve narrative clarity. Scenes can benefit from stronger internal conflict and clearer emotional progression.

refined: Screenwriting: Is beginning to research and develop ideas. Advice to gather specific detail and pre-visualise each scene shot by shot, writing it as concrete action — focused R&D and strong visual writing come through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Demonstrates awareness of dramatic tension and pacing, though rhythm and escalation require refinement.
refined: Screenwriting: Shows awareness of dramatic tension and pacing. Advice to escalate stakes scene by scene and vary scene length to control rhythm — confident pacing and escalation come through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Uses non-linear elements such as ellipsis or reverse chronology, though transitions can be unclear. Focus on making each temporal shift purposeful and clearly signposted — the reader must always know where they are in the story.
refined: Screenwriting: Uses non-linear elements such as ellipsis and reverse chronology. Advice to make each temporal shift purposeful and clearly signposted so the reader always knows where they are — clarity in non-linear work comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Montage sequences are functional, though integration of sound and image can be more purposeful.
refined: Screenwriting: Montage sequences are functional. Advice to decide what each montage must achieve emotionally, then pair image with sound so they amplify rather than merely accompany each other — purposeful montage comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Shows understanding of different formats, though distinctions between film, television, and web writing are not always precise. Analyse the specific structural and tonal conventions of each format — precision here will strengthen the authenticity of the work.
refined: Screenwriting: Understands that formats differ. Advice to study the specific structural and tonal conventions of film, television, and web writing and write to each one's logic — this precision, which strengthens authenticity, comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Web series bible is structured, though episodic arcs and long-term narrative development need further clarity.
refined: Screenwriting: The web series bible is structured. Advice to clarify each episode's arc and chart how the story builds across the season — clear long-term development comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Participates in collaborative development, though contribution to idea expansion can be more defined.
refined: Screenwriting: Participates in collaborative development. Advice to build actively on others' pitches by adding a concrete next beat or complication — a more defined contribution to the room comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Dialogue is functional but occasionally expository; subtext and visual storytelling are present but not consistently sustained. Work more on communicating emotions through visuals and subtext instead of relying only on dialogues.
refined: Screenwriting: Dialogue is functional but sometimes explains too much. Advice to practise saying less in dialogue and showing more through action and image, letting subtext carry the emotion — this restraint develops through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Demonstrates basic understanding of storytelling techniques, though their application remains superficial. Devise techniques to deploy them with intention — how structure, character, and theme interact is what gives a script depth.
refined: Screenwriting: Has a basic grasp of storytelling techniques. Advice to deploy each with clear intent and notice how structure, character, and theme interact — that interplay, which gives a script depth, develops through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Attempts to create dramatic tension, but pacing and rhythm are uneven and lack control. Analyze how scene length and beat placement build and release tension — controlled pacing is the engine of dramatic engagement.
refined: Screenwriting: Is starting to build dramatic tension. Advice to study how scene length and beat placement build and release tension, then revise to control that rhythm — pacing, the engine of engagement, develops through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Use of non-linear storytelling is unclear, affecting narrative coherence. Each departure from chronology must earn its place — Rationalize each time-shift that serves the story's emotional or thematic logic.
refined: Screenwriting: Non-linear storytelling is still developing. Advice to make every departure from chronology earn its place by serving the story's emotional or thematic logic, and to signpost it clearly — coherent non-linear writing comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Montage sequences are attempted but lack clear purpose or integration of sound elements. Explore what each montage needs to achieve emotionally before writing it, and consider how sound will amplify — not merely accompany — the image.
refined: Screenwriting: Is starting to attempt montage. Advice to decide what each montage must achieve emotionally before writing it, then design sound to amplify rather than merely accompany the image — purposeful montage comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Limited understanding of writing across formats, with inconsistent structural approach. Analyse the distinct conventions of film, television, and web writing — each format has its own structural logic.
refined: Screenwriting: Is beginning to write across formats. Advice to study the distinct conventions of film, television, and web writing and apply each one's structural logic — format fluency comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Web series bible is underdeveloped, with weak episodic structure and unclear character arcs. Focus more on episode flow and giving each character a clearer journey.
refined: Screenwriting: The web series bible is in early development. Advice to shape each episode's flow and give every main character a clear journey across the series — a stronger bible comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Limited contribution to collaborative processes, with ideas lacking development. Work on participating more consistently and building further on both your own ideas and others’ inputs.
refined: Screenwriting: Is beginning to take part in collaborative work. Advice to contribute regularly and build on others' pitches by adding a concrete next beat or twist — a stronger room presence comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Dialogue is direct and expository, with minimal subtext; scenes rely more on telling than visual storytelling. Practise writing scenes where the dialogue says less than what is happening — subtext and visual action communicate more powerfully than exposition.
refined: Screenwriting: Dialogue is currently direct and explanatory. Advice to practise scenes where characters say less than they mean and the image carries the meaning — subtext and visual storytelling grow stronger through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Needs stronger understanding and application of storytelling techniques, with more purposeful use of narrative devices and structural choices.
refined: Screenwriting: Is developing a toolkit of storytelling techniques. Advice to use each narrative device and structural choice with a clear reason for what it adds — purposeful technique comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Would benefit from clearer dramatic tension through stronger conflict, pacing, and scene progression to sustain audience engagement.
refined: Screenwriting: Is starting to build dramatic tension. Advice to give each scene clear conflict, escalate the stakes, and control pacing so engagement holds — sustained tension comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Needs further practice in applying non-linear structures with clarity, so that shifts in time and narrative remain purposeful and coherent.
refined: Screenwriting: Is starting to explore non-linear structures. Advice to give each time-shift a clear story reason and signpost it so the reader stays oriented — purposeful, coherent non-linear writing comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Montage sequences would benefit from clearer visual progression and more effective integration of sound, rhythm, and narrative intent.
refined: Screenwriting: Is beginning to write montage. Advice to plan a clear visual progression and decide how sound and rhythm carry the narrative intent — effective montage comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Needs stronger awareness of format-specific writing conventions, with clearer structural approaches suited to different mediums.
refined: Screenwriting: Is beginning to write for different formats. Advice to study the conventions of film, television, and web writing and apply each one's structure — format fluency comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Web series bible would benefit from stronger episodic structure and clearer season-level progression to improve overall coherence.
refined: Screenwriting: The web series bible is at an early stage. Advice to shape each episode's arc and map how the story builds across the season for overall coherence — a strong bible comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Needs continued practice in core writing fundamentals, particularly in reducing expository dialogue and strengthening subtext, visual writing, and scene purpose.
refined: Screenwriting: Is building core writing fundamentals. Advice to cut explanatory dialogue, let images and subtext carry meaning, and give each scene a clear purpose — these fundamentals strengthen through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Would benefit from more consistent application of Semester IV concepts, with greater attention to structure, tension, and execution.
refined: Screenwriting: Is consolidating the Semester IV concepts. Advice to apply structure, tension, and clean execution consistently across each draft — this consistency comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Demonstrates strong control over non-linear, oneiric, and non-dramatic structures, using fragmentation and temporal shifts to enhance meaning rather than disrupt clarity.
refined: Screenwriting: Controls non-linear, oneiric, and non-dramatic structures well, using fragmentation and temporal shifts to enhance meaning rather than disrupt clarity.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Applies adaptation with clear intertextual awareness, transforming source material through reinterpretation of theme, character, and context rather than direct replication.
refined: Screenwriting: Adapts with genuine intertextual awareness, transforming source material through reinterpretation of theme, character, and context rather than direct replication.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Engages real-life events with narrative depth and ethical sensitivity, balancing factual grounding with dramatic construction and character perspective.
refined: Screenwriting: Engages real-life events with depth and ethical sensitivity, balancing factual grounding with dramatic construction and character perspective.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Develops sharp advertisement concepts, with concise one-liners and clearly visualised narrative hooks suited to short-form storytelling.
refined: Screenwriting: Develops sharp advertisement concepts, with concise one-liners and clearly visualised hooks well suited to short-form storytelling.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Generates strong feature-length ideas, identifying concepts with clear dramatic potential, thematic coherence, and narrative scalability.
refined: Screenwriting: Generates strong feature-length ideas, identifying concepts with clear dramatic potential, thematic coherence, and scalability.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Synopses are well-structured, with defined arcs, clear stakes, and progression that reflects feature-length storytelling logic.
refined: Screenwriting: Writes well-structured synopses with defined arcs, clear stakes, and progression that reflects feature-length storytelling logic.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Demonstrates critical understanding of how structure shapes meaning, effectively evaluating and employing non-linear and non-dramatic forms in relation to theme and audience engagement.
refined: Screenwriting: Shows a critical understanding of how structure shapes meaning, evaluating and employing non-linear and non-dramatic forms thoughtfully in relation to theme and audience.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Shows strong conceptual clarity in selecting and developing feature ideas, with clear awareness of scalability, narrative sustainability, and adaptation potential.
refined: Screenwriting: Selects and develops feature ideas with strong conceptual clarity, showing clear awareness of scalability, sustainability, and adaptation potential.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Shows good understanding of non-linear and experimental structures, though transitions and narrative clarity are inconsistent.
refined: Screenwriting: Understands non-linear and experimental structures well. Advice to signpost each time-shift clearly so the reader always knows where they are — clean transitions in non-linear work come through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Demonstrates awareness of adaptation, though reinterpretation remains close to the source and lacks deeper transformation.
refined: Screenwriting: Shows awareness of adaptation. Advice to transform the source more boldly by re-asking its theme through a new context, character, or point of view — meaningful reinterpretation develops through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Engages with real-life material, though ethical framing and narrative perspective require further development.
refined: Screenwriting: Engages with real-life material. Advice to decide whose perspective the story is told from and to weigh how real people are represented — thoughtful ethical framing develops through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Advertisement concepts are clear, though one-liners lack sharpness and visual execution can be more defined.
refined: Screenwriting: Advertisement concepts read clearly. Advice to sharpen the one-liner to a single memorable hook and storyboard the visual beat-by-beat — concise, visually defined ads come through repeated drafting and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Feature-length ideas show promise, though narrative scope and thematic focus need refinement.
refined: Screenwriting: Feature-length ideas show promise. Advice to define the central theme and trim the scope to what one feature can hold — focused, scalable ideas come through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Synopses present a coherent structure, though character arcs and escalation of stakes are uneven. Map each character's arc and the escalation of stakes explicitly before writing — unevenness here weakens the emotional drive of the whole narrative.
refined: Screenwriting: Synopses are coherently structured. Advice to map each character's arc and the rising stakes explicitly before drafting so the emotional drive stays even — this planning habit strengthens through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Demonstrates awareness of feature development requirements, though ideas require further depth and long-form sustainability.
refined: Screenwriting: Shows awareness of what feature development demands. Advice to test each idea for enough conflict and subplot to fill a feature, deepening theme and stakes so it sustains long-form — this judgement develops through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Shows understanding of structural choices, though connection between form, theme, and audience impact is not consistently realised. Refine story structure so that it supports both the theme and the overall emotional journey more effectively.
refined: Screenwriting: Understands structural choices. Advice to align structure with theme and the audience's emotional journey, choosing each form because it serves the story — this connection develops through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Demonstrates basic understanding of non-linear or alternative structures, though execution leads to confusion. Work on improving clarity and progression so that the audience can follow the narrative more easily.
refined: Screenwriting: Has a basic grasp of non-linear structures. Advice to clarify each time-shift and keep an underlying through-line so the audience can follow easily — clarity in alternative structures comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Demonstrates basic understanding of non-linear or alternative structures, though execution leads to confusion. Need more motivation for each structural departure to not to loose grip.
refined: Screenwriting: Has a basic grasp of non-linear structures. Advice to give each departure from chronology a clear story reason and signpost it so the reader stays oriented — controlled non-linear writing comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Engagement with real-life events is superficial, with limited consideration of ethical or narrative positioning. Work on approaching real-life material with greater depth, clarity, and sensitivity.
refined: Screenwriting: Is beginning to engage with real-life events. Advice to research the material closely and decide whose perspective frames the story and how real people are portrayed — depth and ethical sensitivity come through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Advertisement writing lacks conceptual clarity, with weak or unfocused central ideas. Develop on focused concepts to improve clarity, engagement, and overall impact.
refined: Screenwriting: Advertisement writing is still finding its focus. Advice to start from one sharp central idea expressed in a single line before adding visuals — focused, engaging concepts come through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Feature-length ideas lack clear premise, with insufficient narrative direction or thematic grounding. Strengthen the central narrative idea so the story feels more focused and cohesive.
refined: Screenwriting: Feature-length ideas are forming. Advice to anchor each in one clear premise and a guiding theme so it has direction and cohesion — focused feature ideas come through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Synopses are loosely structured, with unclear arcs and weak sense of progression. Work on creating clearer story flow and stronger connections between major plot points.
refined: Screenwriting: Synopses are loosely structured at present. Advice to lay out the major plot points in order and link each to the next as cause and effect — clear arcs and progression come through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Limited understanding of structural intent, resulting in form that appears arbitrary or disconnected from meaning. Refine structural decisions so the storytelling feels more intentional and cohesive.
refined: Screenwriting: Structural intent is still developing. Advice to choose each structural decision because it serves the theme or emotional journey, not for its own sake — intentional, cohesive form comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Feature concepts show limited awareness of scale, with ideas that do not sustain long-form development. Refine the premise and narrative complexity so that the story feels strong enough for feature-length development.
refined: Screenwriting: Feature concepts are emerging. Advice to test each idea for enough conflict, subplot, and theme to fill a feature, then deepen the premise accordingly — long-form judgement comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Needs stronger understanding of non-linear and experimental structures, with greater clarity in how form supports storytelling and audience engagement.
refined: Screenwriting: Is beginning to explore non-linear and experimental structures. Advice to choose each form because it serves the story and keep it clear for the audience — confident experimental writing comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Would benefit from deeper engagement with source material, focusing on interpretation, adaptation choices, and narrative recontextualisation.
refined: Screenwriting: Is beginning to adapt source material. Advice to interpret the source closely, make deliberate adaptation choices, and recontextualise its theme in a new setting or viewpoint — strong adaptation comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Real-life narratives need clearer framing and stronger consideration of perspective, context, and ethical implications within the storytelling.
refined: Screenwriting: Is beginning to write from real life. Advice to choose whose perspective frames the story, set the context clearly, and weigh how real people are represented — thoughtful real-life storytelling comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Feature-length ideas need further development in premise, stakes, and narrative direction to demonstrate stronger long-form potential.
refined: Screenwriting: Feature-length ideas are forming. Advice to anchor each in a clear premise, define what is at stake, and chart its direction — stronger long-form potential comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Advertisement concepts would benefit from a more clearly defined central idea, target audience, and communicative intent.
refined: Screenwriting: Is beginning to shape advertisement concepts. Advice to start by naming the single central idea, the target audience, and what it must communicate before writing — focused ad concepts come through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Synopses would benefit from clearer structure, narrative progression, and stronger focus on character movement and dramatic development.
refined: Screenwriting: Synopses are beginning to form. Advice to order the major beats clearly and track each character's movement and the rising drama through them — well-structured synopses come through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Needs further clarity in applying structural intent, ensuring that formal choices feel purposeful and meaningfully connected to the narrative.
refined: Screenwriting: Is developing a sense of structural intent. Advice to choose each formal decision because it serves the story's theme or emotional journey — purposeful structure comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Feature concepts would benefit from stronger long-form thinking, with greater attention to narrative sustainability and development scope.
refined: Screenwriting: Feature concepts are emerging. Advice to test each idea for enough conflict, subplot, and theme to sustain a whole feature before developing it — long-form thinking grows through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Demonstrates strong clarity in premise and central conflict, establishing a focused narrative direction for the short film.
refined: Screenwriting: Establishes a clear premise and central conflict, giving the short film a focused narrative direction.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Constructs a well-paced 20-minute script, with precise beat structuring, scene progression, and control over narrative rhythm.
refined: Screenwriting: Constructs a well-paced 20-minute script with precise beat structuring, strong scene progression, and confident control of narrative rhythm.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Demonstrates critical comparative analysis of a screenwriter’s work, identifying differences in voice, structure, and thematic concerns.
refined: Screenwriting: Offers critical, comparative analysis of a screenwriter's work, clearly identifying differences in voice, structure, and thematic concerns.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Applies insights from screenwriter study to their own writing, reflecting awareness of voice, form, and narrative choices.
refined: Screenwriting: Applies insights from screenwriter study to their own work, showing real awareness of voice, form, and narrative choices.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Expands a one-page idea into a detailed synopsis, with clear escalation of stakes and narrative layering.
refined: Screenwriting: Expands a one-page idea into a detailed synopsis, with clear escalation of stakes and confident narrative layering.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Develops a feature treatment that sustains narrative progression, with coherent arcs and tonal consistency.
refined: Screenwriting: Develops a feature treatment that sustains narrative progression, with coherent arcs and consistent tone.

original: Screenwriting: Designs episodic structures with distinct episode arcs, ensuring continuity while advancing an overarching narrative.
refined: Screenwriting: Designs episodic structures with distinct episode arcs, sustaining continuity while advancing the overarching narrative.

original: Screenwriting: Constructs a comprehensive show bible, clearly presenting concept, world, character grid, and season-level progression.
refined: Screenwriting: Constructs a comprehensive show bible that clearly presents concept, world, character grid, and season-level progression.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Shows good understanding of premise, though the central conflict requires sharper definition.
refined: Screenwriting: Understands premise well. Advice to define the central conflict in one sentence — who wants what, and what stands in the way — so the story focuses sharply, a clarity that comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Develops a structured short film script, though pacing and scene progression require refinement.
refined: Screenwriting: Develops a structured short film script. Advice to refine pacing by checking that each scene advances the story and tightening transitions between them — smoother progression comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Shows awareness of authorial voice, though its application to their own writing is inconsistent.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Expands ideas into a synopsis, though narrative escalation and depth need further development.

subject: screenwriting

subject: screenwriting

refined: Screenwriting: The show bible communicates the core idea. Advice to expand the world with concrete rules and detail, and to map how the story grows across the season — a robust bible comes through repeated practice and feedback.

subject: screenwriting
original: Screenwriting: Demonstrates basic understanding of premise, though the central idea lacks clarity and focus. Strengthen the premise so the narrative feels more cohesive and easy to follow.

subject: screenwriting
